key_insight: The paper shows that a steady-state 2D induction heating model can be rigorously solved and approximated with finite elements even when the source term is only weakly integrable, and that a bound-preserving heat solver relaxes mesh and domain restrictions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more reliable numerical foundation for simulating induction heating processes in manufacturing and thermal design, especially when standard FEM assumptions are too restrictive.
method: Establish the weak formulation using a priori regularity for the coupled PDEs.
Prove convergence of standard Galerkin FEM on convex domains with suitable meshes.
Apply a bound-preserving method (BPM) to the heat equation to obtain convergence under weaker domain/mesh assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports several numerical experiments that confirm the theory and demonstrate improved performance of the bound-preserving method over standard FEM.
watch_item: The strongest convergence guarantees still depend on domain geometry and mesh conditions, so practical implementation must check these assumptions carefully.
paper_title: Analysis and approximation of a two-dimensional induction heating problem
